div.p:
During 1951, the Board of Directors of the Export-Import Bank authorized $1,000,000 credits to two Bolivian mining companies to finance the cost of machinery, supplies, and equipment in the United States for the expansion of tungsten production. The first credit, approved
November 1, was to Mauricio Hochschild, S. A. M. I. (824.10/11–251), and the second, approved December 19, was to the Bolivian Tin & Tungsten Mines Corporation (824.10/12–2051). For further information, see Export-Import Bank of Washington, Thirteenth Semiannual Report to Congress for the Period July–December 1951 (Washington, 1952), pages 10–11.
